mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) raises inflation expectations, compelling bond investors to demand higher term premiums on long-term debt, thereby pushing the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC between CPIAUCSL month-over-month change and 20-day forward yield change is below 0.05, or the positive prediction success rate in expansion regimes falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeds 5.0%, the Federal Reserve is likely to adopt an accommodative stance by cutting the federal funds rate, which reduces expected real rates and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, when UNRATE > 5.0%, the 20-day forward yield decline does not occur in at least 60% of the instances, or the average yield change is not negative with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated industrial production (INDPRO > 100) and elevated consumer prices (CPIAUCSL > 240) indicate an overheating economy with strong real activity and inflation pressure, prompting bond markets to price in tighter monetary policy and driving the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, the 20-day forward yield change is positive in less than 55% of cases over a 5-year period, or the interaction coefficient in a linear model is not statistically positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%), a high level of housing starts (HOUST > 1000) signals robust residential investment and consumer demand, which increases expectations of future economic growth and eventual monetary tightening, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1% and HOUST > 1000, the 20-day forward yield increase occurs in fewer than 60% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or the average yield change is not positive with a one-sided t-test p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and aggregate demand, leading the Federal Reserve to eventually raise interest rates; this forward-looking expectation pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward yield change is below 0.03, or the directional accuracy over the same period is less than 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) exceeds 17,000 billion chained dollars, the economy is operating at a level that signals above-potential growth, increasing the probability of Fed tightening and raising the equilibrium real rate, thereby driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 > 17000, the 60-day forward yield change is positive in less than 60% of quarters over a 10-year period, or the mean yield change is not positive with p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) and moderately rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 0.5%) characterizes a late-cycle expansion where the labor market is tight and the central bank is cautiously tightening; this fuels expectations of further rate hikes and higher real yields, lifting the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the 20-day forward yield change is positive in fewer than 55% of instances over a 5-year period, or the interaction term in a regression is not positively significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of stable monetary policy (no change in FEDFUNDS over the prior month), an increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals genuine supply-side growth that is not being offset by tightening, leading to higher long-term growth expectations and a rising 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where the FEDFUNDS level has not changed in the prior month, the rank correlation between INDPRO changes and 20-day forward yield changes is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy is below 55%.
